summary: A comprehensive implementation-resolved blueprint that maps the concept layer's abstract entities into concrete packages, files, state slices, actions, routes, and WebSocket messages. Strong structural coverage and clear package boundaries, but the exhaustive enumeration style reduces activation density in the middle sections, and only one end-to-end behavioral flow (bootstrap) is traced despite the scenarios layer defining seven.
strengths: Package dependency graph with explicit ownership and rejection rules creates unambiguous boundary enforcement an agent can act on directly
Full bidirectional action-to-transport mapping (actions call/send routes/messages, messages drive actions) makes the client-server data flow locally recoverable without reading source
Concrete file paths throughout make the blueprint verifiable against the actual codebase
The prescribes block distills nine high-value architectural invariants that compress the concept layer's truths into implementable rules
Consistent abstraction level — never drifts into concept philosophy or raw code details
weaknesses: The 28-entry Action enumeration and six State.updatedBy blocks are catalog-like — high completeness but low activation preload per token compared to grouped semantic summaries
Only the bootstrap flow is traced as a when-then sequence; the edit round-trip, external change, and conflict resolution flows from the scenarios layer are not operationalized end-to-end here
Some file entries use glob paths (e.g. components/*, renderers/*) which reduces precision and makes boundary verification ambiguous

No failure-mode or degradation traces — the concept layer surfaces conflicts and the scenarios layer defines conflict resolution, but this file does not trace how the implementation handles failure paths
